proposed_from.topic: Joint identification and matrix-completion estimator for staggered-treatment panels with low-rank unit-time heterogeneity and an explicit recovery-threshold phase transition expressed in observed eigen-gap and treatment-design constants
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue prior is panel_sdid_mc_id_v1, which targeted spectral equality frontiers, penalty-stable equality, and an SCM/ASCM/SDiD/matrix-completion span lattice. This v5 proposal instead introduces the two-surface target-nullspace defect a_E(P,D), observable separability certificate sigma_obs(P,D), and the separable-completion Riesz map pi_sep for distinguishing low-rank untreated and treatment-effect surfaces. The repo gap remains clear because no banked panel proposal states an iff target-nullspace separability frontier or a two-surface first-order law.","published_axis":"The closest published comparators are FariasLiPeng2021 and AgarwalDahlehShahShen2023 for treatment-effect learning and causal matrix completion, plus ChoiKwonLiao2024, ChoiYuan2024, YanWainwright2024, ChernozhukovHansenLiaoZhu2019, and ZhuLiaoHansenChernozhukov2026 for inference after low-rank completion or low-rank heterogeneous-effect estimation. The epsilon-gap is that those papers estimate missing potential-outcome entries, treated-entry averages, or low-rank slope effects; they do not give an iff scalar target-nullspace frontier or an all-cell Riesz law for separating two low-rank causal surfaces under a deterministic staggered-treatment mask."}
seed_list: Introduce the staggered spectral recovery margin Gamma_r(P,D) and conjecture an iff phase transition for rank-r recovery and MC-SDiD-ASCM first-order equality under deterministic staggered masks.
Characterize the penalty-stable equality set across ridge ASCM, SDiD quadratic penalties, and nuclear-norm MC using observed weak-factor singular gaps and mask leverage constants.
Prove a strict SCM/ASCM/SDiD/MC exact-identification lattice with finite Schur/Riesz certificates for adjacent ATT equality and separation.
Construct an orthogonal-score estimator combining SDiD balancing and low-rank nuisance recovery, with double robustness to either balancing restrictions or rank recovery.
Give uniform event-time confidence bands after low-rank imputation with coverage constants expressed in observed eigen-gap and staggered-design constants.
Characterize separability of a low-rank treatment-effect surface from low-rank untreated interactive fixed effects under staggered observation masks.
Give an iff recovery boundary for matrix completion with no never-treated units, using later-treated cohort support and anchor-block eigen-gaps.
Promote the deterministic staggered-mask nonrecovery result to an open-class partial-identification bound for treated counterfactual cells below the spectral margin.
Define an adaptive tuning rule for causal-panel matrix completion whose rank and penalty choices are identification-neutral exactly above an observed eigen-gap threshold.
Bridge random-mask matrix-completion phase transitions and econometric staggered-adoption panels by proving a deterministic-mask reduction with cohort-overlap constants.

seed_details.one_line: Introduce the staggered spectral recovery margin Gamma_r(P,D) and conjecture an iff phase transition for rank-r recovery and MC-SDiD-ASCM first-order equality under deterministic staggered masks.

anchor_paper.author: Athey, Bayati, Doudchenko, Imbens, Khosravi; Candes and Tao; Keshavan, Montanari, Oh; prior CausalSmith panel_sdid_mc_id
anchor_paper.claim: Causal-panel MC gives estimators; classical MC gives random-mask thresholds; prior reviewer requested an explicit finite-dimensional observable computation.
anchor_paper.location: Stage -1.1 gap open_problems[0]; panel_sdid_mc_id angle0_v5 strengthening_paths[1]

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends random-mask phase transitions to staggered masks, but non-trivial because it adds a deterministic observed margin and estimator-law equality, not a routine mask swap.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from classical matrix completion because deterministic masks can be checked by tangent-space injectivity.
seed_details.rebuttal: Classical injectivity is not an observed staggered-design eigen-gap certificate and does not imply SDiD/ASCM/MC first-order equivalence.

adversarial_referee.instance: The seed needs a named focal object, not an abstract recovery condition.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Gamma_r(P,D) is named and defined as an observed SVD/design margin.

adversarial_referee.instance: The positive-margin proof may only give sufficiency.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Necessity is explicitly Conjecture 1 and paired with an open-class below-threshold Conjecture 2.

adversarial_referee.instance: Below-threshold witness could be a hand-tuned collapsed cohort.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The draft requires a three-cohort four-period non-corner witness and relative-open promotion.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize the penalty-stable equality set across ridge ASCM, SDiD quadratic penalties, and nuclear-norm MC using observed weak-factor singular gaps and mask leverage constants.

anchor_paper.author: Heiniger; Bai and Ng; Moon and Weidner; prior CausalSmith panel_sdid_mc_id
anchor_paper.claim: Model selection and factor imputation leave open when tuning paths are identification-neutral in weak-factor staggered panels.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Penalty-path extension is stronger because weak factors can change the estimand, producing an exceptional-set formula rather than a tuning heuristic.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just standard perturbation theory for penalized estimators.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap is joint equality of three named causal-panel tuning paths as a function of observed staggered mask constants.

adversarial_referee.instance: No estimator-rate package is named.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Package would include equality of first-order laws across the tuning paths.

adversarial_referee.instance: A sufficient stability interval may be incremental.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs an exceptional-set or iff frontier; otherwise downgrade to field.

adversarial_referee.instance: Heiniger may already cover selection stability.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The gap context says Heiniger lacks finite-sample eigen-gap identification thresholds.

seed_details.one_line: Prove a strict SCM/ASCM/SDiD/MC exact-identification lattice with finite Schur/Riesz certificates for adjacent ATT equality and separation.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith panel_sdid_mc_id
anchor_paper.claim: Strict span lattice was marked NOT YET PROVED and determinant-ratio formulas may need adjustment.
anchor_paper.location: Stage -1.1 gap open_problems[2]; prior proposal §8 and §11

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Decompose/equality-lattice shape is non-trivial only if finite certificates separate every adjacent named estimator node.
seed_details.referee_objection: This repeats the failed panel_sdid_mc_id proposal.
seed_details.rebuttal: As a seed it would need new Schur/Riesz certificates and minimal bounded-rank witnesses; otherwise it should be dropped.

adversarial_referee.instance: The prior banked angle was rejected.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Only viable if it fixes the exact reviewer failure with finite certificates.

adversarial_referee.instance: SCM/ASCM/SDiD/MC may target different objects by construction.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Seed must define common ATT functional and adjacent equality certificates.

adversarial_referee.instance: Strictness witnesses may be rank-deficient corner cases.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Requires open bounded-rank families, not single examples.

seed_details.one_line: Construct an orthogonal-score estimator combining SDiD balancing and low-rank nuisance recovery, with double robustness to either balancing restrictions or rank recovery.

anchor_paper.author: Arkhangelsky, Athey, Hirshberg, Imbens, Wager; Ben-Michael, Feller, Rothstein; Roth and Sant'Anna
anchor_paper.claim: Existing SDiD and staggered SCM results do not provide a joint low-rank/balancing orthogonal score.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends estimator classes, but non-trivial if it proves a double-robust moment and efficiency bound rather than an algorithm benchmark.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is DML language pasted onto SDiD.
seed_details.rebuttal: The contribution would be a specific orthogonal score for staggered low-rank panels and a named efficiency bound.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs an estimator and rate, not just identification.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The seed explicitly includes orthogonal-score estimator and efficiency bound.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only one ATT could be stated.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Must cover cohort-time/event-time ATT family jointly.

adversarial_referee.instance: Double robustness may require strong assumptions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Flagship only if one nuisance can be misspecified under stated alternatives.

seed_details.one_line: Give uniform event-time confidence bands after low-rank imputation with coverage constants expressed in observed eigen-gap and staggered-design constants.

anchor_paper.author: Yan and Wainwright; Choi, Kwon, Liao; Cattaneo, Feng, Palomba, Titiunik
anchor_paper.claim: Entrywise low-rank and synthetic-control inference do not yet give dynamic event-study bands with shared recovery constants.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Inference extension is stronger if coverage constants reuse the recovery margin and handle cohort aggregation.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an inference add-on to existing matrix-completion bands.
seed_details.rebuttal: The gap is simultaneous dynamic ATT bands under staggered missingness with observable eigen-gap/design constants.

adversarial_referee.instance: Identification may be inherited, leaving only inference.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Must couple bands to a recovery certificate; otherwise downgrade to field.

adversarial_referee.instance: Cohort-time and event-time contrasts must be handled jointly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State uniform bands for the full contrast family.

adversarial_referee.instance: Yan-Wainwright may already cover staggered missing panels.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Gap context says dynamic event-study aggregation and recovery constants remain missing.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize separability of a low-rank treatment-effect surface from low-rank untreated interactive fixed effects under staggered observation masks.

anchor_paper.author: Sun and Abraham; de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille; Farias, Li, Peng
anchor_paper.claim: Heterogeneous treatment effects and low-rank treatment-effect learning do not characterize joint recoverability of two low-rank matrices.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Target variation is strong because it exposes entanglement hidden by untreated-only MC.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is generic nonidentification of two latent matrices from one observed matrix.
seed_details.rebuttal: The staggered mask and low-rank treatment-effect surface create a specific causal separability frontier.

adversarial_referee.instance: Nonidentification could use a zero treatment arm.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs relative-open nonzero treatment-effect surfaces.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could identify only one average effect.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State separability for the whole treatment-effect surface or named family.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs a focal object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use a separability rank/mask certificate as focal object.

seed_details.one_line: Give an iff recovery boundary for matrix completion with no never-treated units, using later-treated cohort support and anchor-block eigen-gaps.

anchor_paper.author: Poulos, Albanese, Mercatanti, Li; Ben-Michael, Feller, Rothstein
anchor_paper.claim: No-never-treated matrix-completion workflows lack a sharp low-rank recovery boundary.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends donor support regimes, but non-trivial if it proves an iff boundary rather than a sufficient overlap rule.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a special case of the deterministic staggered recovery margin.
seed_details.rebuttal: It is a publishable corollary or fallback, but less broad than seed 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could only prove enough later-treated overlap.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs necessity via indistinguishable completions.

adversarial_referee.instance: No estimator-rate duality.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: ExactID may pass if iff boundary is sharp; panel flagship stronger with estimator duality.

adversarial_referee.instance: Poulos et al. may already propose the workflow.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: They do not supply the sharp rank/eigen-gap recovery theorem per gap context.

seed_details.one_line: Promote the deterministic staggered-mask nonrecovery result to an open-class partial-identification bound for treated counterfactual cells below the spectral margin.

anchor_paper.author: Candes and Tao; Keshavan, Montanari, Oh; prior CausalSmith panel_sdid_mc_id
anchor_paper.claim: Classical recovery thresholds do not give causal partial-identification bounds below deterministic staggered-mask failure.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/impossibility is strong only with open-class promotion and nondegenerate observed laws.
seed_details.referee_objection: Below-threshold nonidentification is obvious from failed completion.
seed_details.rebuttal: Sharp partial-ID bounds for the causal ATT cells are not obvious and need an evaluable support-function object.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs computable identified set, not just nonidentification.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would use support-function or extremal-completion representation.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could rely on a single missing block.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Must prove relative-open nonrecovery class.

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims M11 but may deliver only tangent nonidentification.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Downgrade unless sharp bound representation is included.

seed_details.one_line: Define an adaptive tuning rule for causal-panel matrix completion whose rank and penalty choices are identification-neutral exactly above an observed eigen-gap threshold.

anchor_paper.author: Heiniger; Bai and Ng; Choi, Kwon, Liao
anchor_paper.claim: Recent selection and inference papers do not give an identification-neutral tuning rule from observed singular gaps.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Tuning-rule seed risks algorithmic benchmark; only non-trivial as identification-neutrality theorem with first-order law.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is model selection, not causal identification.
seed_details.rebuttal: Would be acceptable only if the rule is tied to a causal ATT identification and influence-law theorem.

adversarial_referee.instance: May lack new identification object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use observed eigen-gap threshold and first-order law as focal objects.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could be a conservative sufficient rule.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs exact neutrality frontier; otherwise field-tier.

adversarial_referee.instance: Rank selection and penalty selection differ.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Must separate rank recovery from penalty active-face stability.

seed_details.one_line: Bridge random-mask matrix-completion phase transitions and econometric staggered-adoption panels by proving a deterministic-mask reduction with cohort-overlap constants.

anchor_paper.author: Candes and Tao; Keshavan, Montanari, Oh; Athey, Bayati, Doudchenko, Imbens, Khosravi
anchor_paper.claim: Random-mask MC thresholds and causal-panel deterministic masks are not bridged by a theorem with cohort-overlap constants.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Bridge shape is useful but weaker than chosen seed unless it also delivers estimator equality.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just translating notation from statistics to econometrics.
seed_details.rebuttal: Translation becomes substantive only if deterministic cohort-overlap constants recover or separate causal ATT cells.

proposed_from.literature_map:
Athey, Bayati, Doudchenko, Imbens, and Khosravi (2021, JASA) anchor nuclear-norm causal-panel matrix completion for treated counterfactual cells.
Bai and Ng (2021, JASA) and Bai (2009, Econometrica) anchor factor-imputation and interactive fixed-effect counterfactual theory.
Arkhangelsky, Athey, Hirshberg, Imbens, and Wager (2021, AER) anchor SDiD as a balancing-plus-factor estimator.
Ben-Michael, Feller, and Rothstein (2022, JRSSB) extend synthetic controls to staggered adoption and emphasize pooled imbalance.
Callaway and Sant'Anna (2021, Journal of Econometrics), Sun and Abraham (2021, Journal of Econometrics), de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille (2020, AER), and Borusyak, Jaravel, and Spiess (2024, ReStud) anchor modern staggered-DID/event-study identification and heterogeneity failures.
Roth and Sant'Anna (2021/2023, arXiv) contribute efficient staggered-rollout estimation under design-style timing assumptions.
Choi, Kwon, and Liao (2024, Journal of Econometrics) and Yan and Wainwright (2024, arXiv) push low-rank completion inference, including staggered-adoption missing panels.
Choi and Yuan (2024, JASA) and Agarwal, Dahleh, Shah, and Shen (2023, COLT/PMLR) connect causal matrix completion to MNAR or latent-confounded missingness.
Candes and Tao (2010, IEEE TIT), Keshavan, Montanari, and Oh (2010, IEEE TIT), and Candes-Recht-style results provide classical recovery-threshold anchors, mostly under random masks.
The main tension is that econometric panel papers give estimators and asymptotics for causal masks, while statistical matrix-completion papers give phase transitions mostly outside deterministic staggered-treatment designs.
The live gap for this qid is a theorem-level bridge: observed eigen-gap and treatment-design constants should determine exact recovery, partial identification, or nonrecovery and should feed directly into tuning and inference.

banked_reason: Stage -1.2 NO-PASS @ flagship (D-1.2 effort=medium, reverted from high). 20 reviewer rounds across 4 angles; tier=flagship reached 4 times but never with all flags (S+N+C) clear simultaneously. Final angle=4 v5 REVISE@field S=2 N=3 C=2. Oscillation pattern: when the recovery-threshold scalar is named concretely (e.g. eigen-gap × treatment-design constant) novelty drops to field; when named abstractly, correctness flags fire. Same shape as runs 1 and 3 (kernel_substituted-adjacent but never reached D-0.5 ACCEPT).
